decisions.reasoning:
* Technical Analysis: Broad market (SPY/QQQ) is in a healthy uptrend with RSI in the 45-57 range (not overbought). Among single names, the strongest trend setups — AAPL (RSI 61.9, +8.1% 20d), JPM (RSI 62.2, +8.8% 20d), and META (RSI 68.7, +17.2% 20d) — are all trading within ~2-5% of their 20-day highs, meaning fresh long entries now would be chasing an already-extended move rather than entering with room to run. META in particular is overbought (RSI 68.7) and up nearly 15% in 5 days alone. GOOGL, MSFT, and XLE are in mild downtrends (all below their 50-day averages) and do not offer a clean long setup either. NVDA and AVGO show strong 5-day momentum but sit close to their own 20-day highs.

* Fundamental Analysis: No earnings, financial statements, or company-specific news were provided for this decision — per the data constraints, only price/volume/technical data is available today.

* Market Context: This is the first decision for this $1000 paper account. Data reflects the most recently completed session (Friday, 2026-07-10); no new session has occurred since (decision run on a non-trading day).

* Risk Assessment: None of the available setups offer a favorable risk/reward for a fresh entry today — the trending names are extended near recent highs (poor reward for the risk of a pullback), and the laggards are in downtrends without a clear reversal signal. Initiating a position here would be chasing strength rather than a high-probability, well-timed entry. Confidence for any single new trade is below the 50 threshold, so per the decision philosophy (protect capital first, HOLD is often correct, cash is a valid position), the correct call is to hold 100% cash and re-evaluate with fresh data on the next trading session, watching for pullbacks toward the 20-day moving averages in AAPL, JPM, or NVDA as potential lower-risk entries.

[Data-source note: this session's network policy blocked the normal pipeline (query1.finance.yahoo.com, news.google.com, www.sec.gov all returned policy-level 403s from the egress proxy), so no fresh OHLCV/technical-indicator/EDGAR data was available. As a fallback, current price/news context was gathered via web search (Google-indexed finance/news pages) instead of the structured scripts/fetch-market-data.mjs + fetch-news.mjs + fetch-fundamentals.mjs pipeline. That fallback is explicitly qualitative, not authoritative: within a single search result the same source restated NVDA's 'current' price two different ways ($211.80 vs $207.57) and MSFT's cited 52-week high ($555.45) is inconsistent with its ~$385 current quote in the same summary. Per the system prompt's risk rules, every trade needs a defensible Entry Price / Stop Loss / Take Profit / Risk-Reward Ratio computed off real data — that bar isn't met by noisy web-search snippets, so no ticker below was sized as a trade regardless of directional view.]

* Technical Analysis (Web Search, approximate — not the usual Yahoo Finance OHLCV feed): AAPL printed a fresh record high (~$321 on 2026-07-13 per TipRanks/Yahoo Finance via search) after a reported +16% run since June 25 — an extended, 'chasing strength' setup rather than a clean entry. NVDA was reported up ~4% on 2026-07-14 (CNBC/web search) but with conflicting exact price figures in the same result. TSLA is described as down >12% year-to-date despite a recent bounce, and JPM/AVGO both look like they already made their move (JPM up on its Q2 print, AVGO near highs on the Apple supply-deal news) rather than offering a fresh entry point.

* Fundamental Analysis (Web Search headlines, not SEC EDGAR XBRL): JPM beat Q2 2026 estimates (reported $16.9B net income, $6.14 EPS, per CNBC/Reuters coverage via search) and popped on the print (2026-07-14) — real positive fundamentals, but already reflected in the price by the time this decision is made. MSFT similarly beat FQ2 2026 estimates (reported $81.3B revenue, +17% YoY, EPS $4.14) with the reaction already priced in. Across AMZN and META, web coverage repeatedly flags very large 2026 AI capex guidance ($125-145B for META; heavy AI/infra spend for AMZN) pressuring near-term free cash flow, a real fundamental headwind for both mega-cap names right now.

* Market Context (Web Search market-news summaries): Broad market (SPY/QQQ) coverage describes a choppy tape driven by Strait of Hormuz/Iran-related oil headlines, partially offset by a cooler-than-expected June CPI print. XLE (~$56.74) sits mid-range between its 52-week low ($42.05) and high ($63.46), tracking oil-driven swings rather than a clean directional trend. Nothing in the available coverage signals a decisive market-wide risk-on or risk-off shift.

* Risk Assessment: Today's binding constraint is data quality, not just setup quality — without the structured technical-indicator pipeline, position sizing, stop-loss, and take-profit levels can't be computed against a real, trustworthy fetched close per the system prompt's mandatory risk fields (2% max risk per trade, defined risk-reward ratio). Combined with the best-known directional candidates (AAPL, NVDA, JPM, AVGO) already having made their moves rather than offering a fresh entry, capital preservation favors HOLD today: cash is a valid position, and no candidate clears the bar for both a sound setup and reliable enough data to size and risk-manage responsibly. Re-evaluate once the structured Yahoo Finance / Google News / SEC EDGAR pipeline is reachable again.
decisions._dataAsOf: 2026-07-14 (web search fallback, approximate — see data-source note)
